title: Tracing & retry settings for next demo run
summary: Keep claim-level trace summaries inline, retain full raw traces on disk (not in the UI), and use a small retry cap (one automatic retry only for transient provider errors) to stay within the 1.15x cost ceiling while preserving reviewer efficiency and demo responsiveness.

claims.text: Use claim-level trace summaries (sources cited, what evidence is missing, retry count, artifact path) inline next to evaluator findings, and avoid showing raw provider payload dumps in the dashboard.
Retain full detailed traces on disk for post-run audit/debug, but keep the live demo view readable via summaries rather than long raw traces.
Keep a strict retry cap: allow one automatic retry for transient provider errors, and log retries (and any resulting cost/latency change) in the run manifest; avoid silent repeated generations.
Target live-demo responsiveness by keeping single briefings under ~10 seconds and showing per-case progress and artifact paths during longer runs.

Manage tracing depth/cost to stay within Finance’s approved 1.15x cost ratio for demo-quality runs; if the run exceeds that ceiling, reduce batch size, cap retries, or summarize traces more aggressively.

openQuestions: Do we expect transient provider errors during the demo environment (which would trigger the single allowed automatic retry), or should we disable auto-retry entirely for the live segment?
Will the next demo run be close to the 1.15x cost ceiling given the current trace verbosity, or do we need to reduce case batch size up front?
Is the UI already able to show current case, elapsed time, and artifact path continuously, or do we need a lightweight progress panel for the demo?
recommendation: For the next demo run: keep trace summaries inline at the claim/finding level and store full raw traces on disk only; cap retries to one automatic retry for transient provider errors with manifest logging; and prioritize perceived responsiveness by keeping single-briefing latency under ~10s while continuously displaying per-case progress and artifact paths. If cost drifts above 1.15x, first tighten trace summarization and retry behavior, or reduce the case batch size.
